steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with 'Though,' setting up a contrast between many prior studies and the researchers' 'novel path.' The blank describes the prior studies, which must be the opposite of novel — standard and expected.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'eccentric': Eccentric means unusual or odd, which would make the prior studies as unconventional as the researchers' new approach — eliminating the contrast entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — 'meager': Meager means scarce or insufficient; the passage says 'many' prior studies exist, so scarcity is contradicted by the text. ✗
  • Choice C — 'conventional': Conventional means following established norms. This creates the precise contrast with 'novel' that 'Though' signals. ✓
  • Choice D — 'random': Random implies no systematic design, which is irrelevant to the contrast the sentence draws. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
